original_datasets.name: wage_gain_table
original_datasets.notes: Dataset used for both Task1 and Task2. Key variables referenced in the R analysis: edyrs (years of schooling), country (country code), lastUsWageAdjusted, lastHomeWageAdjusted. PPP-adjustment details are in the original paper. The reanalyst text mentions removing Peru as an outlier, but the provided code omits the Peru dummy rather than removing those rows.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Create schooling and country dummies, compute wage gain as absolute difference between US-adjusted and home-adjusted wages, regress wage gain on schooling and country dummies without intercept.
preprocessing_steps: Create schooling dummies: vNS (edyrs<9), vSHS (edyrs in 9,10,11), vHSD (edyrs=12), vSC (edyrs in 13,14,15), vGRAD (edyrs>=16).
Create country dummies: vCOL, vDOM, vECU, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vPER, vSLV.
Drop rows with missing values in used columns.

key_choices.outcome_measure: vWage: absolute difference between lastUsWageAdjusted and lastHomeWageAdjusted.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Schooling category dummies (vNS, vSHS, vHSD, vSC).
control_variables: Country fixed effects via dummies (vCOL, vDOM, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vSLV).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Peru excluded from the country dummies; no explicit row removal.
key_choices.outlier_rule: Peru treated as outlier by omission from dummies; otherwise not_stated.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Rows with missing values in used columns are dropped.
key_choices.data_processing: Dummy coding, computation of absolute wage difference.

main_predictor.name: vNS, vSHS, vHSD, vSC
main_predictor.definition: Schooling category indicators constructed from edyrs.

controls.name: vCOL, vDOM, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vSLV
controls.definition: Country fixed effects via country dummies.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Use the pooled sample of immigrants from poor countries in the NIS and the Migration Projects, including Mexico. Do not control for sector and region.

analysis_path.path_description: Create schooling and country dummies, compute wage gain as absolute difference between US-adjusted and home-adjusted wages, regress wage gain on schooling and country dummies with intercept. Perform joint F-test for vSHS=vHSD=vSC=0. Estimate reduced model with vNS and country dummies.
preprocessing_steps: Create schooling dummies: vNS (edyrs<9), vSHS (edyrs in 9,10,11), vHSD (edyrs=12), vSC (edyrs in 13,14,15), vGRAD (edyrs>=16).
Create country dummies: vCOL, vDOM, vECU, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vPER, vSLV.
Drop rows with missing values in used columns.

key_choices.outcome_measure: vWage: absolute difference between lastUsWageAdjusted and lastHomeWageAdjusted.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Schooling category dummies (vNS, vSHS, vHSD, vSC).
control_variables: Country fixed effects via dummies (vCOL, vDOM, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vSLV).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Peru excluded from the country dummies; no explicit row removal.
key_choices.outlier_rule: Peru treated as outlier by omission from dummies; otherwise not_stated.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Rows with missing values in used columns are dropped.
key_choices.data_processing: Dummy coding, computation of absolute wage difference.
key_choices.model_choice: linear regression (OLS) with intercept; joint F-test of schooling dummy coefficients.
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value threshold from OLS coefficient tests; joint F-test for vSHS=vHSD=vSC=0.

main_predictor.name: vNS, vSHS, vHSD, vSC
main_predictor.definition: Schooling category indicators constructed from edyrs.

controls.name: vCOL, vDOM, vGTM, vHTI, vMEX, vNIC, vSLV
controls.definition: Country fixed effects via country dummies.

analysis_code.notes: Reads /app/data/wage_gain_table.xlsx. Outputs under /app/data/Task2_543X6__py/: full_model_coef.csv, reduced_model_coef.csv, joint_test.txt.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned linear regression path and joint F-test as described; do not introduce sector or region controls.

files.data/Analysis_543X6__py.py: Python translation of Task1 R analysis. Loads dataset from /app/data, builds schooling and country dummies, computes wage gain, runs OLS without intercept, writes coefficient table.
files.data/Task2_543X6__py.py: Python translation of Task2 R analysis. Loads dataset from /app/data, builds dummies, computes wage gain, runs OLS with intercept, performs joint F-test for vSHS=vHSD=vSC=0, fits reduced model, writes outputs.

codebase.notes: The original analyses were in R Markdown (Analysis_543X6.Rmd and Task2_543X6.Rmd). They have been translated to Python to comply with execution policy. Ensure the Excel file is available at /app/data/wage_gain_table.xlsx or mount the local data folder accordingly.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset and Python translations. Ensure /app/data contains wage_gain_table.xlsx. Capture model coefficients, p-values, and the Task2 joint F-test results.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: OLS coefficients and p-values for schooling dummies; Task2 joint F-statistic and p-value for vSHS=vHSD=vSC=0; reduced-model coefficients; conclusion direction (wage gain decreases with schooling).
